title: Revisiting the Blackstone Fortress
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=T2tuMBwhuKc

After a week of properly digging into the base-box campaign, Daniel (@dungeondive) returns to Warhammer Quest: Blackstone Fortress with a more considered take. The game's biggest strength, he argues, is its sense of time and place — as strong as anything he can think of — built through the lore booklet, the Darius Hinks tie-in novel, and gorgeous cards and minis. The MMO-style loop of randomly-generated expeditions to gather clues, then raids on four strongholds before a final hidden vault, is genuinely compelling.

But that strength shades into the game's main flaw: the core campaign runs perhaps 40 hours on too little variety, with combat and enemy types repeating before the length earns itself. Daniel's fix is the Strikeforce rules from the White Dwarf 2019 annual, which let him play two heroes instead of four — his sweet spot — by handing out extra destiny dice rather than rescaling enemies. He praises the constantly-shifting initiative track, the one-use ship items, and the near-perfect number of per-turn decisions, all inherited from the Silver Tower foundation he considers a masterpiece. His plan: rush the base game and move into the tighter, better-paced expansions.

----

Daniel thinks the boxed expansions' shorter, self-contained campaigns beat the sprawling base game. Do you prefer a long single campaign, or several tight self-contained ones?
